title: Indian Shuttler Rounak Chouhan Defeats World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a significant upset by defeating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open.
summary: Rounak Chouhan, an Indian shuttler and Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a major upset at the US Open Super 300, beating the higher-ranked Chou Tien Chen.

title: BAI Issues Advisory on Unauthorized Badminton Leagues and Tournaments
indian_angle: Badminton Association of India has issued a public advisory against unauthorized badminton leagues and tournaments, emphasizing official sanctioning.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has released an advisory warning players and officials about participating in unsanctioned badminton leagues and tournaments across the country.

title: Further Addendum to Selection Criteria for Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships
indian_angle: This addendum details the updated selection process for India's junior players aiming for the prestigious Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has published a further addendum to the selection criteria for the upcoming Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships 2026, outlining revised guidelines for junior players.

title: PV Sindhu's Early Career Path to Stardom Began in Macau
indian_angle: The article explores the initial stages of Indian badminton icon PV Sindhu's journey to international fame, highlighting her early experiences in Macau.
summary: A feature piece delves into the formative years of PV Sindhu's career, tracing her first steps towards becoming a global badminton superstar, with a focus on her time in Macau.

title: Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games; Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: The core of India's successful Thomas Cup squad is regrouping, with the notable return of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, as they prepare for the Asian Games.
summary: India's badminton contingent for the Asian Games sees the reassembly of key players from the Thomas Cup winning squad, including the return of the women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.

title: India Invites Expression of Interest to Host BWF World Championships 2026
indian_angle: India has initiated the process to potentially host the prestigious BWF World Championships in 2026, inviting expressions of interest.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued an Expression of Interest (EOI) for organizing the BWF World Championships 2026, signaling India's ambition to host the global event.
